channel.title: Phoenix Contact
channel.description: Latest Phoenix Contact news feed snapshot with title, link, date, and description.

item.title: Phoenix Contact sells Perle Systems Ltd. to Hitron Technologies Inc.
item.description: Phoenix Contact has reached an agreement to sell Perle Systems Ltd. and its subsidiaries to Hitron Technologies Inc. The transaction represents an important step in Phoenix Contact's strategic focus on its core businesses.

item.title: First phase of construction for Phoenix Contact’s new Mexican production facility in Querétaro officially inaugurated
item.description: To strategically expand Phoenix Contact’s global production network, improve supply chains, and be even closer to its target markets, the company has expanded its production network on the American continent. Production has now begun at the site in Querétaro, Mexico, as part of the first phase of construction.

item.title: Phoenix Contact and Symcon enter into a technology partnership for open building automation
item.description: To further advance open, interoperable building automation, Phoenix Contact and Symcon have entered into a technology partnership. The goal of this collaboration is to provide planners, system integrators, and electrically skilled persons with an efficient path from hardware through software to ongoing operation.

item.title: The Emalytics building management system is the key to the digital transformation in facility management
item.description: The long-term goal is to make all building data available centrally and consistently throughout its entire lifecycle. This requires a fully digitalized engineering process in which information is entered only once, stored in a structured manner, and processed across systems.

item.title: Surge protection for the busbar with integrated voltage pick-off
item.description: The new FLT-SEC-ZP2-…-SV combined lightning current and surge arrester from Phoenix Contact sets new standards in the protection of meter panels: The low overall width of the surge protection leaves plenty of space for the other components mounted on the busbar.

item.title: New safety relay module for potentially explosive areas
item.description: With the new PSR-MC35-EXI safety relay, Phoenix Contact is further expanding its safety portfolio. The product enables safe operation in demanding industrial environments. It was specifically developed for use in zone 2 potentially explosive areas.

item.title: Sunclix DC connectors approved for 2,000 V DC in accordance with new TÜV test specification
item.description: The Sunclix DC connectors from Phoenix Contact are now approved for a system voltage of up to 2,000 V DC in accordance with the TÜV Rheinland 2 PfG 2925/01.23 test specification.

item.title: Phoenix Contact E-Mobility undergoes strategic realignment and exits the automobile OEM business
item.description: Phoenix Contact E-Mobility has reached an important milestone in its strategic transformation with its planned exit from the passenger car business (OEM automotive). As part of an asset deal, the TE Connectivity Group is acquiring significant assets and activities in connection with Phoenix Contact E-Mobility’s business with passenger car manufacturers.
